pattern: A multiset can be rearranged into a palindrome exactly when at most one of its values occurs an odd number of times, so tracking odd/even parity per digit along a path is enough -- no permutation ever needs to be built.
hints: You don't need to try rearranging anything -- a sequence is 'palindrome-able' exactly when at most one of its values has an odd count.
Walk root to leaf carrying a running parity for each of the nine possible digit values (odd-so-far or even-so-far), flipping the current digit's parity at each node.

A bitmask of 9 bits (one per digit 1..9) toggled with XOR at every node does this in O(1) per step; at a leaf the path qualifies exactly when the mask has zero or one bit set, i.e. mask & (mask - 1) == 0.

edge_inputs.description: single node is trivially a palindrome path
every path has exactly two distinct odd-count digits, so none qualify
long single-branch path where the same digit repeats an even number of times
all nine digits appear once each down one path

stress.description: random binary tree with digit values 1-9, moderate branching
stress.note: digits restricted to 1-9 per the constraint
